description: Pie chart of North Carolina students by 2025 proficiency level, all subjects and grades, with slices labeled directly: Level 1 or 2 is the largest share at 45 percent, then Level 4 (24), Level 3 (20), and Level 5 (11). Level 1 or 2 — students below grade level — is colored rust so the nearly-half share reads as a single block; the three proficient levels are neutral grey, with white separators.

title.text: Nearly half of NC students scored below grade level
subtitle: Share of NC students by 2025 achievement level, all subjects and grades. Below grade level = Level 1 or 2 (below GLP, Level 3+).
Pies suit part-to-whole with five slices or fewer — order and label them directly.

layer.comment: Two label layers at constant radius (150 for most, 168 for Level 1 or 2 so it clears its neighbours). Using two layers + opacity — not a radius field encoding — keeps the inherited theta stack intact so labels stay matched to their slices.

usermeta.source: Source: NCDPI accountability test scores (achievement levels, all subjects and grades, all students), 2025. Style-guide demo.
